calls.query: {"strategic_intent": "Read the prompt contract and agent guidelines to understand the prediction task.", "summary": "I am starting by reading the prediction prompt contract (.github/prompts/predict.md) and the agent guidelines (AGENTS.md) to understand the requirements, expected output files, and the rules for generating a prediction.", "title": "Analyze Prediction Guidelines"}

{"strategic_intent": "Resolve paths for the provisioned inputs for this specific prediction cell.", "summary": "I am running the `fedcourts paths` command as instructed by the predictor guidelines to get the exact file paths for the case facts and where I should write my prediction outputs.", "title": "Retrieve Case Paths"}

{"strategic_intent": "Read the introduction and reasons for granting the petition to assess the strength of the petitioner's argument.", "summary": "I am reading the beginning of the petition for writ of certiorari to identify the key arguments made by the petitioner, particularly whether they allege a split among lower courts on the issue of independent contractors under the Rehabilitation Act. This split is often the primary reason the Supreme Court grants a petition.", "title": "Evaluate the

{"strategic_intent": "Read the statistical base rates from the corpus to anchor the prediction mathematically.", "summary": "I am reading the project's statpack.md file to determine the base rate for cert grants, specifically looking at the base rate for paid petitions where a response was requested but no relists or CVSG have occurred yet. I will also check the salience bands to see how the statistical model rates cases with these signals.", "title": "Review Statistical Base Rates"}
grep -A 30 "Segment base rate by salience band" metrics/statpack.md
grep -A 50 "## SCOTUS cert petitions by Term" metrics/statpack.md
grep -A 10 "## Cert petitions by CVSG status" metrics/statpack.md
grep -A 10 "## Cert petitions by relist count" metrics/statpack.md

{"strategic_intent": "Run a query on the corpus to find prior SCOTUS cases dealing with Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act and independent contractors.", "summary": "I am using the `fedcourts query` tool to search the corpus for similar historical petitions involving the Rehabilitation Act, Section 504, and independent contractors. This will help determine the baseline grant rate for this specific legal issue and whether the Supreme Court typically grants certiorari to resolve splits on this
uv run fedcourts query --court scotus "Rehabilitation Act independent contractors section 504"

{"strategic_intent": "Use the CourtListener MCP search tool to investigate the circuit split regarding the Rehabilitation Act and independent contractors.", "summary": "Since the corpus query tool doesn't support full-text search, I am using the `mcp_courtlistener_search` tool to search for opinions discussing Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act and independent contractors. This will confirm the existence and depth of the circuit split claimed in the petition, informing the likelihood of cert

{"strategic_intent": "Find the salience band base rates in the statpack to properly calculate the probability of a grant.", "summary": "I need to look up the exact base rate for the petition's salience band in `metrics/statpack.md`, as instructed by the predictor guidelines. I am using `grep` to find the term \"salience\" in the file to locate the correct table.", "title": "Look Up Salience Base Rates"}
